folio.label: 84r
folio.l: Come there , the nedetú nat , of me to lere
For æè shalt , be thyn owen ex¹ience
Come in a chayer , rede al this sentence
Bette than virgile , while he was on lyue
Or Dant also , now late vs ride blyue
For I wyl holde , companye witú the
Til it be so , that thow forsake me
¢ Nay q¶ this Sompnoå , that shal nat betide
I am a ©eman , that knowen is ful wyde
My trouthe wol I holde , as in this caas
For thougú thow were , the deuele Sathanas
My trouthe wyl I holde , to the my brother
As I haue sworn~ , and ecú of vs to other
For to be trewe bretheren , as in this cas
And bothe we gon , a bouten oure purchas
Take thow thy part , what æ æ men wol the ©eue
And I shal myn , thus may we bothe leue
And if that any of vs , haue more than other
Lat hi~ be trewe , and part it witú his brother
¢ I gunte q¶ the Deuele , be my fey
And witú that word , they ryden fortú here wey
And rigút at the entryngö , of the tounes ende
To whicú this Sompnoå , shop~ hi~ for to wende
They saw a cart , that charged was witú hey
Whicú that a Cartere , drof fortú in his wey
Depe was the weye , for whicú the cart stode
This Cartere smot , and cried as he were wode
Hayt Brok hayt Skot , what spare ©e for the stones
The fend q¶ he , ©ow fecche body and bones
As ferfortú , as euäe were ©e foled
So mechil woo , as I haue witú ©ow tholed
The deuele haue al , bothe hors Cart and hey
¢ This Sompnoå seide , here shuln we han a pley
And nere the fend he drow , as nougút ne were
Ful pryuely , and rovned in his Eere
Herkene my brother , herken be thyn feitú
Herest thow nat how , that the Cartere seitú
Hent it a noon , for he hatú ©eue it the
Bothe hey and cart , and eke hise caples three
Nay q¶ the deuele , god wot neuäe a dele
It is nat his entent , trost me wele
Aske hi~ thy selue , if æè nat trowest me
Or ellis stynt a while , and æè shalt se
This Cartere takkitú his hors , on the croupe
And they bygonne , to drawen & to stoupe
Hayt now q¶ he , there iúu cést ©ow blisse
And al his handwerk , bothe more & lesse
That was wel twyt , myn owen Lyard boy
